title: Export to Excel - issue w/ format - broken Google Drive compatibility

posts.author: juanmaldonado (Juan Maldonado)
posts.cookedHtml:
Recent changes to the export to excel feature broke compatibility when uploading the xlsx file to Google Drive. The problem is the format.
Please revert to 2007 Excel format.
This is only happening with normal boards, not with multi-level boards.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @juanmaldonado !

It may help to report this through official support so the team can look into it. Export behavior can sometimes change after updates, and issues like file format compatibility usually need to be reviewed on their side.

In the meantime, a practical workaround is opening the exported file in Excel and saving it again before uploading to Google Drive, or importing the file into Google Sheets directly rather than uploading it as an Excel file.

If you contact support, sharing an example file and noting that it only affects normal boards (not multi-level boards) should help them reproduce the issue more easily.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Apparently the root cause of this is that sometime in February, monday updated their code to use zip64 encoding to create the xlsx file zip archive. Google Drive cannot open xlsx files that are zip64 encoded.

The real fix for this therefore is to get monday to revert their zip encoding to the previous version, or to get Google to read both formats.

If this affects you, open a support ticket with monday or Google, or both.
